mission.title: caddy: No matching certificate to load: decoding certificate metadata: invalid character '}'
mission.description: No matching certificate to load: decoding certificate metadata: invalid character '}'. This issue affects 8+ users.

steps.title: Identify the corrupt certificate metadata error
steps.description:
Check Caddy logs for the certificate metadata decoding error:
```
caddy[1208]: {"level":"error","logger":"tls.on_demand","msg":"loading newly-obtained certificate from storage","remote_ip":"192.0.2.1","remote_port":"49984","server_name":"server.example.com","error":"no matching certificate to load for server.example.com: decoding certificate metadata: invalid character '}' after top-level value"}
```
This error indicates a corrupt JSON metadata file in Caddy's certificate storage directory.

steps.title: Locate the corrupt certificate metadata file
steps.description:
Find the certificate metadata files in Caddy's data directory. The default location is `~/.local/share/caddy/certificates/` or the path configured via `storage file_system { root ... }`:
```bash
find /var/lib/caddy/.local/share/caddy/certificates -name '*.json' \
  -exec sh -c 'python3 -m json.tool "$1" > /dev/null 2>&1 || echo "CORRUPT: $1"' _ {} \;
```
This scans all certificate metadata JSON files and reports any that fail to parse.

steps.title: Delete the corrupt metadata file and regenerate
steps.description:
Remove the corrupt metadata file for the affected domain. Caddy will automatically re-obtain the certificate and regenerate valid metadata on the next TLS handshake:
```bash
# Back up the corrupt file first
cp /path/to/corrupt-metadata.json /tmp/corrupt-metadata.json.bak
# Remove the corrupt metadata
rm /path/to/corrupt-metadata.json
# Restart Caddy to trigger certificate re-obtainment
systemctl restart caddy
```

steps.title: Verify certificates are loading correctly
steps.description:
After restarting Caddy, verify that the certificate is obtained and loaded without errors:
```bash
# Check Caddy logs for successful certificate obtainment
journalctl -u caddy --since '5 minutes ago' | grep -i certificate
# Test TLS connection to the affected domain
curl -vI https://server.example.com 2>&1 | grep 'SSL certificate'
```
Confirm the decoding error no longer appears in the logs.

resolution.summary: The certificate metadata corruption (invalid JSON character '}') was caused by a corrupt or hand-edited certificate metadata file in Caddy's data directory. The fix in Caddy v2.9.0 improved JSON parsing resilience for certificate metadata files. Workaround: delete the corrupt metadata file and let Caddy regenerate certificates.
codeSnippets:
# Find and remove corrupt metadata files
find /var/lib/caddy/.local/share/caddy/certificates -name '*.json' \
  -exec sh -c 'python3 -m json.tool "$1" > /dev/null 2>&1 || echo "CORRUPT: $1"' _ {} \;
# Then delete the corrupt file and restart Caddy
systemctl restart caddy

prerequisites.description: A working caddy installation or development environment.
